repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
## Problem

If `UV_PROCESS_DETACHED` is not set when using `uv_spawn`, which is called by `vim.system`, the child process does not get its own process group and it therefore becomes impossible to kill its descendants, resulting in #39552.

## Solution

This PR simply makes `vim.system` mimic the internal functions called by `jobstart` (`libuv_proc_spawn` and `os_proc_tree_kill`).

comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -452,7 +453,7 @@ local function run(cmd, opts, on_exit)
     cwd = opts.cwd,
     --- @diagnostic disable-next-line:assign-type-mismatch
     env = setup_env(opts.env, opts.clear_env),
-    detached = opts.detach,
+    detached = sysname == "Windows_NT" and opts.detach or true,

comment_data.body:
"win32" is the documented way to check for windows (and it will return true for all windows versions, not just "32bit"). not sure that "win" works.
```suggestion
    detached = vim.fn.has("win32") == 1 and opts.detach or true,
```

-    detached = opts.detach,
+    detached = vim.fn.has("win") == 1 and opts.detach or true,

 function SystemObj:kill(signal)
-  self._state.handle:kill(signal)
+  local pid = self._state.pid
+  if vim.fn.has("unix") == 1 then

comment_data.body:
just guessing, but Windows CI still failing, try keeping the old code there.

```lua
if vim.fn.has('win32') == 0 then
  uv.kill(-pid, signal)
else
  self._state.handle:kill(signal)
end
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -103,7 +103,11 @@ end
 ---
 --- @param signal integer|string Signal to send to the process. See |luv-constants|.
 function SystemObj:kill(signal)
-  self._state.handle:kill(signal)
+  local pid = self._state.pid --[[@as integer]]
+  if vim.fn.has('win32') == 0 then
+    pid = -pid
+  end
+  uv.kill(pid, signal)

+  local pid = self._state.pid --[[@as integer]]
+  if vim.fn.has('win32') == 0 then
+    pid = -pid

comment_data.body: This is broken. `opts.detach` should be respected if the user provides it. This code forces `detached` to `true` on unix.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -452,7 +456,7 @@ local function run(cmd, opts, on_exit)
     cwd = opts.cwd,
     --- @diagnostic disable-next-line:assign-type-mismatch
     env = setup_env(opts.env, opts.clear_env),
-    detached = opts.detach,
+    detached = vim.fn.has('win32') == 1 and opts.detach or true,

comment_data.body:
that's the only way for group signaling to work, bc of libuv limitation: https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/d788dd2811bfbfb0daa915ef974d072075a6bceb/src/nvim/event/libuv_proc.c#L40-L41

however I agree that we probably still want to give user control over this.

comment_data.body: I agree that a docs update is required, but the behavior is not documented for `jobstart` either so I figured I'd update them both at once (in one patch) later.

comment_data.body: `-pid` is how you pass a PGID to POSIX `kill`
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -103,7 +103,11 @@ end
 ---
 --- @param signal integer|string Signal to send to the process. See |luv-constants|.
 function SystemObj:kill(signal)
-  self._state.handle:kill(signal)
+  local pid = self._state.pid --[[@as integer]]
+  if vim.fn.has('win32') == 0 then
+    pid = -pid

comment_regular_data.body:
For reference, there is similar logic in `vim._transport`.
https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/329dc30ef09c785b6577bae7fcbcfe44b7d9057f/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/_transport.lua#L46-L50
I assume this implementation is intended to align with platform-specific characteristics?

On unix-likes the `detach` is necessary for the child process to get its own process group, which is necessary for it and its descendants to be killable as one unit. I am not sure how Windows behaves, with or without it, I only copied the existing behavior (from `libuv_proc_spawn`)

worth a try, thanks. cc @lewis6991 

but the lint issues need to be fixed. (`lintcommit` noticed that the commit msg needs a blank line after the subject).


... and Windows CI is failing:

```
FAILED   .../build/Xtest_xdg_lua/test/functional/lua/system_spec.lua:124: .../build/Xtest_xdg_lua/test/functional/lua/system_spec.lua @ 41: vim.system (async) supports timeout
.../build/Xtest_xdg_lua/test/functional/lua/system_spec.lua:124: .../build/Xtest_xdg_lua/test/functional/lua/system_spec.lua:41: process did not exit
stack traceback:
	test/functional/testnvim/exec_lua.lua:124: in function 'system'
	.../build/Xtest_xdg_lua/test/functional/lua/system_spec.lua:124: in function <.../build/Xtest_xdg_lua/test/functional/lua/system_spec.lua:118>
```

@justinmk I didn't realize there was a different implementation of `os_proc_tree_kill` for windows, so I assumed that passing `-pid` to uv_kill would just "do the right thing" there, but it doesn't, and that's what was causing the test failure. It's fixed now, and the patch isn't touching the windows path at all anymore.

The implementation of `os_proc_tree_kill` for windows calls win32 directly and as such it would be difficult to rewrite in Lua for `vim.system`. It would indeed be ideal if it just started calling the C functions.

CI still failing. you can use `make lintlua lintc` locally... 

> The implementation of `os_proc_tree_kill` for windows calls win32 directly and as such it would be difficult to rewrite in Lua for `vim.system`. It would indeed be ideal if it just started calling the C functions

my reply is still the same: https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/39552#issuecomment-4363910406
Sorry about that. Thanks for your patience.

title: fix(jobs): always detach on unix
